stemHtml: A fundamental difference between the manoeuvring limit load factor and the gust limit load factor is, that:

options.a: the manoeuvring limit load factor is independent of the size of the aeroplane, whereas the numerical value of the gust limit load factor is laid down in the regulations.
options.b: the gust limit load factor can be higher than the manoeuvring limit load factor.
options.c: the gust limit load factor has a fixed value for every transport aeroplane, whereas the manoeuvring limit load factor has not.
options.d: the gust limit load factor is independent of the wing’s aspect ratio, whereas the manoeuvring limit load factor is not.
